info.title: Prism — Touch-accessible in-column reorder
info.description: This feature adds NO new API endpoints. It is a frontend-only change: two new buttons (move up / move down) in CardActionMenu call the already-existing rank-update contract below, computing the target rank client-side via computeDropRank() exactly as HTML5 drag-and-drop already does. This document exists to make the reused contract explicit for the developer stage, per ADR-1 and blueprint.md §3.2/§3.4.

patch.summary: Update a task's rank within its column (REUSED — no change)
patch.description: Existing endpoint, unchanged. Currently invoked by HTML5 drag-drop (Board.handleDrop) and, after this feature, also invoked by the new move-up/move-down buttons via Board.handleReorderStep. Both call sites compute `rank` the same way, via computeDropRank().

rank.description: New fractional rank. For a one-step move, computed by computeDropRank() against the adjacent rank-neighbor in the sorted column array — same algorithm already used by drag-drop, now also used by the up/down buttons.

200.description: Task updated with its new rank.

500.description: Persist failed. Frontend rolls back the optimistic reorder and shows an error toast (see wireframes.md, Error state).

Task.description: Unchanged task shape. Only `rank` changes as a result of this feature.

suggestion.example: The card may have been deleted or moved. Reload the board to see its current position.

REORDER_FAILED_UI_CONTRACT.description: Frontend-only contract (not an HTTP error code): when reorderTask's PATCH rejects, useAppStore.reorderTask rolls back the optimistic move and calls showToast with the friendly message below. No new error code is introduced server-side.
REORDER_FAILED_UI_CONTRACT.toastMessage: Couldn't save the new order. Please try again.

x-reused-contracts: computeDropRank() — frontend/src/... rank computation, reused verbatim for the up/down step
useAppStore.reorderTask — optimistic update + persist + rollback + toast, reused verbatim
